mission: Coordinate AE brain capabilities, donor-derived project generation, provider contracts, proof artifacts, and design-vault references so SkyDexia can create complete user projects autonomously.

lanes.purpose: AE brain conversation and task handling
Create project specs, register capabilities, generate project outputs, and preserve provenance
Use SkyeHands design vault sources and recipes to raise frontend, 3D, and UX quality
Convert donor code and enterprise references into trusted, provenance-preserved project templates
Generate websites, SaaS UIs, app shells, 3D web experiences, and AE-ready client delivery packages through SkyeWebCreatorMax
Send generated website/UI artifacts to AE CommandHub for productization and client delivery
Screenshot generated artifacts with headless Puppeteer at 1440x900. Saves preview.png to the project artifacts directory. Called automatically as Step 6 of every build.

Full website build with Three.js forced on. Uses Three.js r169 CDN, WebGLRenderer alpha+antialias, pixel ratio, resize listener, slow animation loop (0.0003x), max 3 meshes, ambient+directional lighting. AE agents use this when the client brief calls for an immersive 3D web experience.

Standard AE-callable build entry point. Pass a brief string, get back a project manifest with siteName, qualityScore, artifactsDir, previewScreenshot. Primary lane for AE agents triggering website generation.
Check worker liveness and provider key availability before dispatching a build job. AE agents should call this before long-running builds to fail fast.

autonomyRules: Use donor references only with provenance and compatibility checks.
Use design-vault references before creating user-facing UI.
Route AE work through registered capabilities and provider contracts.
Run smoke/proof scripts or report blockers before claiming completion.
Every website build must complete Step 6 (browser preview) or log the specific reason it was skipped.
Three.js builds must use the r169 CDN, try/catch guard, and pixel ratio — no bare THREE usage.
